author: Twifies
text:
Creating A World That Doesn't Exist
Searching for a behind the scenes that doesn't exist...

GET THE MAPS (NOW ON BEDROCK!!): https://horacecreations.com/products/dtl

Portrait of God: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BI9fKfX5V68

Tale Foundry: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B-s3YxqCt6g

Hana edits tiktok: https://www.tiktok.com/@hanaaaeditzzz


I mentioned in this video that I won't be talking about ALL the references/easter eggs/inspirations for Destroying A World That Doesn't Exist, and that's for two reasons. For one, there would just be way too many to fit in one video. But for two, I think certain themes/ideas work better when I don't tell you exactly what I intended so you as the viewer and project and interpret the video in a way that's more meaningful to you. I don't want to spoil any interpretation by giving you my intended vision. The best video is one that can be read in multiple ways. 

HOWEVER if you do want to see an extended QnA where I talk about more niche stuff then you can here: https://youtu.be/gCvGC6QrTB8

(Also this description accidentally deleted itself an hour ago? Not sure how that happened lol

What is Roblox? ROBLOX is an online virtual playground and workshop, where kids of all ages can safely interact, create, have fun, and learn. It’s unique in that practically everything on ROBLOX is designed and constructed by members of the community. ROBLOX is designed for 13 to 18 year olds, but it is open to people of all ages. Each player starts by choosing an avatar and giving it an identity. They can then explore ROBLOX — interacting with others by chatting, playing games, or collaborating on creative projects. Each player is also given their own piece of undeveloped real estate along with a virtual toolbox with which to design and build anything — be it a navigable skyscraper, a working helicopter, a giant pinball machine, a multiplayer “Capture the Flag” game or some other, yet-to-be-dreamed-up creation. There is no cost for this first plot of virtual land. By participating and by building cool stuff, ROBLOX members can earn specialty badges as well as ROBLOX dollars (“Robux”).

author: VALORANT Champions Tour Americas
text:
SEN vs NRG - VCT Americas Stage 1 - Week 5 Day 3
VCT Americas Stage 1 marks the next step in the race to Champions Shanghai, and the stakes could not be higher. Twelve teams, two groups, and only three spots on the line.

Group Alpha is fueled by one central clash. Brazil versus the Americas. LOUD, MIBR led by Aspas, and FURIA bring regional pride, while Leviatán and Cloud9 look to challenge them. G2 stands in the middle, ready to prove they still belong among the elite.Group Omega is shaped by pressure. NRG stand as the favorites, but KRÜ arrive ready to fight, and Envy reenters the spotlight with legendary names looking to prove they can still compete.

Only 3 teams will reach Masters London. Tune in to find out Americas representatives!

We’re proud to work with our fantastic partners throughout 2026: ExpressVPN, HP, Pulsar, Red Bull, Samsung, Secretlab, and Verizon.

Follow the below channels to get the latest information on VCT Americas:
Twitter/X: https://www.x.com/valesportsna

author: The Spiffing Brit
text:
POV: You're Playing the Hardest 2007 Flash Game
Today we return to the glorious golden age of Flash gaming to investigate Age of War, a perfectly balanced 2007 strategy classic with absolutely no exploits whatsoever. Except, of course, for the bit where we completely break it.

With nothing but questionable tactics, historical absurdity, and a suspicious quantity of tea, we shall guide humanity from cavemen throwing rocks to futuristic death machines deleting entire timelines. What begins as a simple battle of bases soon becomes a deeply British exercise in economic abuse, turret nonsense, unit spam, and making the game regret ever giving us control.

Is Age of War balanced? Probably not. Is it still one of the greatest Flash games ever made? Absolutely. Can we ruin it using strategy so cursed it belongs in a museum? Naturally.

Put the kettle on, prepare the nostalgia, and witness the most civilised act of digital warfare since someone discovered the special attack button.

Age of War is a perfectly balanced game with no exploits.

Insta: https://www.instagram.com/realfoltyn/

Twitter: https://twitter.com/Foltynnn

What is Roblox? ROBLOX is an online virtual playground and workshop, where kids of all ages can safely interact, create, have fun, and learn. It’s unique in that practically everything on ROBLOX is designed and constructed by members of the community. ROBLOX is designed for 13 to 18 year olds, but it is open to people of all ages. Each player starts by choosing an avatar and giving it an identity. They can then explore ROBLOX — interacting with others by chatting, playing games, or collaborating on creative projects. Each player is also given their own piece of undeveloped real estate along with a virtual toolbox with which to design and build anything — be it a navigable skyscraper, a working helicopter, a giant pinball machine, a multiplayer “Capture the Flag” game or some other, yet-to-be-dreamed-up creation. There is no cost for this first plot of virtual land. By participating and by building cool stuff, ROBLOX members can earn specialty badges as well as ROBLOX dollars (“Robux”). In turn, they can shop the

author: Twifies
text:
Creating A World That Doesn't Exist
Searching for a behind the scenes that doesn't exist...

GET THE MAPS (NOW ON BEDROCK!!): https://horacecreations.com/products/dtl

Portrait of God: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BI9fKfX5V68

Tale Foundry: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B-s3YxqCt6g

Hana edits tiktok: https://www.tiktok.com/@hanaaaeditzzz


I mentioned in this video that I won't be talking about ALL the references/easter eggs/inspirations for Destroying A World That Doesn't Exist, and that's for two reasons. For one, there would just be way too many to fit in one video. But for two, I think certain themes/ideas work better when I don't tell you exactly what I intended so you as the viewer and project and interpret the video in a way that's more meaningful to you. I don't want to spoil any interpretation by giving you my intended vision. The best video is one that can be read in multiple ways. 

HOWEVER if you do want to see an extended QnA where I talk about more niche stuff then you can here: https://youtu.be/gCvGC6QrTB8

(Also this description accidentally deleted itself an hour ago? Not sure how that happened lol
